original_abstract: Abstract Epoxides are significant intermediates for the manufacture of pharmaceuticals and epoxy resins. In this study, we develop a Br − /BrO − mediated photoelectrochemical epoxidation system on α-Fe 2 O 3 . High selectivity (up to >99%) and faradaic efficiency (up to 82 ± 4%) for the epoxidation of a wide range of alkenes are achieved, with water as oxygen source, which are far beyond the most reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical epoxidation performances. Further, we can verify that the epoxidation reaction is mediated by Br − /BrO − route, in which Br − is oxidized non-radically to BrO − by an oxygen atom transfer pathway on α-Fe 2 O 3 , and the formed BrO − in turn transfers its oxygen atom to the alkenes. The non-radical mediated characteristic and the favorable thermodynamics of the oxygen atom transfer process make the epoxidation reactions very efficient. We believe that this photoelectrochemical Br − /BrO − -mediated epoxidation provides a promising strategy for value-added production of epoxides and hydrogen.
humanized_abstract: Abstract Abstract Abstract Abstract Abstract Epoxides are important precursors to many pharmaceuticals and epoxy resins, etc. In this study, a Br − /BrO − mediated photoelectrochemical system for the selective and efficient photoelectrochemical epoxidation of α-Fe 2 O 3 towards α-unsaturated aldehydes, naphthalene derivatives, alkyl oleate esters, alkyl fumarate esters, arylalkenes, styrene and acetophenone was established by adjusting the Br − /BrO − concentration, light intensity, alkaline solution concentration and pH in aqueous solution. High selectivity (up to >99%) and faradaic efficiency (up to 82 ± 4%) were achieved for the targets within a broad range of alkenes with water as oxygen source, which is far beyond the most reported electrochemical and photoelectrochemical epoxidation performances. Moreover, the Br − /BrO − mediated pathway for the photoelectrochemical selective and efficient epoxidation of alkenes has been verified. Br − is oxidized non-radically by an oxygen atom transfer pathway to produce BrO − on the α-Fe 2 O 3 photoanode surface, and the as-formed BrO − is responsible for the subsequent oxygen atom transfer to alkenes for producing targeted epoxides. The non-radical mediated characteristic and favorable thermodynamics of the oxygen atom transfer contribute significantly to the high efficiency of the photoelectrochemical selective epoxidation of alkenes, thereby providing a new photoelectrochemical Br − /BrO − -mediated method for efficient value-added production of various chemicals such as hydrogen and epoxides.
